key_insight: The paper proposes a fully model-free deep learning framework for solving high-dimensional nonlinear parabolic PDEs by replacing explicit derivative computation with zeroth-order derivative estimators from simulated trajectories.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical PDE learning in black-box scientific and reinforcement-learning settings where system dynamics are unknown, unstable to differentiate, or only accessible through simulation.
method: Use two simulator types to generate pointwise samples and perturbed Monte Carlo trajectories.
Train solution, gradient, and Hessian networks with zeroth-order derivative targets instead of automatic differentiation.
Derive non-asymptotic error bounds that separate discretization, approximation, statistical, and ZOD bias terms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theoretical statistical learning analysis and numerical experiments showing competitive performance in moderate and high dimensions.
watch_item: ZOD estimators introduce bias-variance tradeoffs and the method relies on a contraction assumption plus simulation access, so performance may depend on perturbation design and simulator quality.
paper_title: A Zeroth-Order Deep Learning Method for Fully Nonlinear Parabolic Partial Differential Equations with Unknown Coefficients
